content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a Tiny Macro Lisp system that compiles to COR24 assembly. Phase 1: lexer and parser. Phase 2: evaluator with core special forms. Phase 3: compiler targeting COR24 assembly. Phase 4: integration with tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain.

=== STEPS ===
  [!] 001-lexer [production]: Implement the Lisp lexer: tokenize parentheses, symbols, numbers, strings, an...
  [x] 002-research-and-plan [production]: Read research, study COR24 toolchain, create architecture/prd/design/plan/stats docs
  [x] 003-scaffold [production]: Set up C source structure based on architecture
  [ ] 003-003-scaffold [legacy]: Set up C source structure: create src/tml.h (Value type, tags, macros, forwar...
  [x] 004-003-scaffold-cor24 [legacy]: Redo scaffold for COR24 target. CC=tc24r, run with cor24-run --run. No libc h...
  [ ] 004-lexer [production]: Implement tokenizer for Lisp syntax
  [x] 005-reader [production]: Implement S-expression reader
  [ ] 005-005-reader [legacy]: Implement S-expression reader in src/read.h. Parse from char* string input. H...
  [x] 006-eval-core [production]: Implement eval with core special forms
  [ ] 007-gc [production]: Implement mark-sweep garbage collector <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 008-compiler [production]: Compile to COR24 assembly
  [ ] 009-integration [production]: Wire to tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain

=== CURRENT STEP: 007-gc [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Implement mark-sweep garbage collector
Task type: c-compile-fix

=== SKILL: c-compile-fix (v1) ===
Fix C compiler warnings and errors without disabling warnings

Procedure:
  1. Run make (with -Wall -Wextra -Werror) to see all warnings
  2. Fix each warning by correcting the code, not by suppressing it
  3. NEVER add #pragma GCC diagnostic ignored
  4. NEVER remove -Werror or -Wall from CFLAGS
  5. Re-run make to confirm zero warnings
  6. Run tests if available to confirm fixes did not break anything

Known failure modes:
  - pragma_suppress (4x): Using #pragma to suppress warnings instead of fixing them
  - void_cast (3x): Casting to void* to silence type warnings instead of fixing types

=== PAST SUCCESSES (c-compile-fix) ===
  [2026-03-23T17:54:40] action=wrote eval.h, updated main.c with comprehensive test suite, built and ran on COR24 emulator, result=success, reward=+1
  [2026-03-23T17:25:31] action=wrote read.h with read_str/read_expr/read_list/read_int/read_symbol functions; fixed fixnum_val in tml.h for logical-shift COR24; added comprehensive reader tests in main.c, result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

file.content:
#pragma once

/* tml.h -- Tiny Macro Lisp core types and macros
 *
 * All values are a single COR24 int (24-bit).
 * Low 2 bits = tag, upper bits = payload.
 *
 *   [23..2] payload  [1:0] tag
 *   00  Fixnum   -- 22-bit signed integer
 *   01  Cons     -- heap cell index
 *   10  Symbol   -- symbol table index
 *   11  Extended -- heap cell index (closure, macro, primitive, string)
 */

/* Tags */
#define TAG_FIXNUM   0
#define TAG_CONS     1
#define TAG_SYMBOL   2
#define TAG_EXTENDED 3
#define TAG_MASK     3

/* Tag checks */
#define IS_FIXNUM(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_FIXNUM)
#define IS_CONS(v)     (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_CONS)
#define IS_SYMBOL(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_SYMBOL)
#define IS_EXTENDED(v) (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_EXTENDED)

/* Fixnum encode/decode */
#define MAKE_FIXNUM(n)  (((n) << 2) | TAG_FIXNUM)
#define FIXNUM_VAL(v)   ((v) >> 2)

/* Pointer (index) encode/decode */
#define MAKE_CONS(idx)     (((idx) << 2) | TAG_CONS)
#define MAKE_SYMBOL(idx)   (((idx) << 2) | TAG_SYMBOL)
#define MAKE_EXTENDED(idx) (((idx) << 2) | TAG_EXTENDED)
#define PTR_IDX(v)         ((v) >> 2)

/* Special values: nil = symbol 0, t = symbol 1 */
#define NIL_VAL   MAKE_SYMBOL(0)
#define T_VAL     MAKE_SYMBOL(1)
#define IS_NIL(v) ((v) == NIL_VAL)

#define HEAP_SIZE 4096

/* Extended object type tags */
#define ETYPE_CLOSURE   MAKE_FIXNUM(1)
#define ETYPE_MACRO     MAKE_FIXNUM(2)
#define ETYPE_PRIMITIVE MAKE_FIXNUM(3)
#define ETYPE_STRING    MAKE_FIXNUM(4)

file.content:
TC24R = tc24r
COR24_RUN = cor24-run
SRC = src/main.c
ASM = build/tml24c.s

all: $(ASM)

$(ASM): $(SRC) src/tml.h src/io.h src/heap.h src/symbol.h src/print.h src/read.h src/eval.h
	@mkdir -p build
	$(TC24R) $(SRC) -o $(ASM)

run: $(ASM)
	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM)

clean:
	rm -rf build

content.text: Now I have a clear picture of the system. Let me implement the mark-sweep GC.

Now I'll create the GC module. The design:

- **Mark bitmap** via `heap_mark[]` array
- **Free list** threaded through `heap_cdr` of swept cells
- **Root stack** for protecting temporaries during allocation
- GC triggers automatically when allocation fails
- `gc_protect`/`gc_unprotect` for temporary root registration

1. Wire gc.h into main.c (after eval.h, since it uses `global_env`)
2. Replace `alloc_cell()` in heap.h with the GC version
3. Add GC tests
4. Update Makefile

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me verify the GC test output shows correct behavior:

- **100 garbage pairs allocated**, heap_next reached 536
- **gc_collect ran** (collections=1), freeing unreachable cells
- **Post-GC allocation works** — `(99)` created from free list
- **Protected value survives GC** — after 2nd collection, val still `(99)`
- **Eval still works after GC** — `(+ 100 200) => 300`
- **"gc ok"** printed

Let me also check that the `(+ 1 2)`, `x`, `(double 21)`, and `(fact 5)` tests pass (they're between collections=1 and post-gc).
All GC tests pass. Closures, recursion, and all global bindings survive garbage collection correctly. Now let me commit and complete the step.

input.command: git add src/gc.h src/heap.h src/main.c Makefile && git diff --cached --stat

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement mark-sweep garbage collector

Adds gc.h with mark-sweep GC: mark bitmap, free list threading through
heap_cdr, root stack (gc_protect/gc_unprotect) for temporaries, and
automatic collection on OOM. Allocation checks free list first, then
bump allocator, then triggers GC. Tests verify reachable data survives
collection and freed cells are reused.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
